sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: κἀνταῦθʼ Ἀπόλλων οὔτʼ ἐκεῖνον ἤνυσεν
sections.translation: So , in that case , Apollo did not bring it to pass that the child should become the slayer of his father , or that Laius should suffer that which he feared , death at the hands of his child : thus the messages of the seer ’ s art had mapped out the future . Pay them no regard . Whatever necessary event

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: χρείαν ἐρευνᾷ , ῥᾳδίως αὐτὸς φανεῖ .
sections.translation: the god seeks , he himself will easily bring to light .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original: οἷόν μʼ ἀκούσαντʼ ἀρτίως ἔχει , γύναι ,
sections.translation: What restlessness of soul , lady , what tumult has come upon me since I heard you speak !

sections.label: Section 728
sections.original: ποίας μερίμνης τοῦθʼ ὑποστραφεὶς λέγεις ;
sections.translation: What anxiety has startled you , that you say this ?

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: ἔδοξʼ ἀκοῦσαι σοῦ τόδʼ , ὡς ὁ Λάϊος
sections.translation: I thought that I heard this from you — that Laius

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: κατασφαγείη πρὸς τριπλαῖς ἁμαξιτοῖς .
sections.translation: was slain where the three roads meet .

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: ηὐδᾶτο γὰρ ταῦτʼ οὐδέ πω λήξαντʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: Yes , that was the report , and so it is still thought .

sections.label: Section 732
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ ʼσθʼ ὁ χῶρος οὗτος οὗ τόδʼ ἦν πάθος ;
sections.translation: And where is the place where this occurred ?

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: Φωκὶς μὲν ἡ γῆ κλῄζεται , σχιστὴ δʼ ὁδὸς
sections.translation: The land is called Phocis ; the branching forks lead to the same spot from Delphi and from Daulia .

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: καὶ τίς χρόνος τοῖσδʼ ἐστὶν οὑξεληλυθώς ;
sections.translation: And how much time has passed since these events took place ?

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: σχεδόν τι πρόσθεν ἢ σὺ τῆσδʼ ἔχων χθονὸς
sections.translation: The news was announced to the town shortly before you first attained power over this land .

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , τί μου δρᾶσαι βεβούλευσαι πέρι ;
sections.translation: O Zeus , what have you decreed for me ?

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστί σοι τοῦτʼ , Οἰδίπους , ἐνθύμιον ;
sections.translation: Why , Oedipus , does this matter weigh upon your heart ?

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: μήπω μʼ ἐρώτα · τὸν δὲ Λάϊον φύσιν
sections.translation: Do not ask me yet . Tell me rather what stature Laius had , and how ripe his manhood was .

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: μέγας , χνοάζων ἄρτι λευκανθὲς κάρα ,
sections.translation: He was tall — the silver just lightly strewn among his hair — and his form was not greatly unlike your own .

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλας · ἔοικʼ ἐμαυτὸν εἰς ἀρὰς
sections.translation: Unhappy that I am ! I think that I have

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: δεινὰς προβάλλων ἀρτίως οὐκ εἰδέναι .
sections.translation: laid myself under a terrible curse without realizing it .

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; ὀκνῶ τοι πρός σʼ ἀποσκοποῦσʼ , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: How do you mean ? I tremble when I look at you , my lord .

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: δεινῶς ἀθυμῶ μὴ βλέπων ὁ μάντις ᾖ ·
sections.translation: I have dread fears that the seer can see . But you will reveal the matter better if you tell me one thing more .

sections.label: Section 749
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὀκνῶ μέν , ἃ δʼ ἂν ἔρῃ μαθοῦσʼ ἐρῶ .
sections.translation: Indeed , though I tremble , I will hear and answer all that you ask .

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: πότερον ἐχώρει βαιὸς ἢ πολλοὺς ἔχων
sections.translation: Did he go with a small force , or like a chieftain , with many armed followers ?

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: κῆρυξ · ἀπήνη δʼ ἦγε Λάϊον μία .
sections.translation: Five they were in all — a herald among them — and there was one carriage which bore Laius .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ὁ τούσδε λέξας τοὺς λόγους ὑμῖν , γύναι ;
sections.translation: Alas ! It is all clear now ! Who gave you this information , lady ?

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: οἰκεύς τις , ὅσπερ ἵκετʼ ἐκσωθεὶς μόνος .
sections.translation: A servant , the only survivor who returned home .

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: ἦ κἀν δόμοισι τυγχάνει τανῦν παρών ;
sections.translation: Is he by any chance in the house now ?

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ · ἀφʼ οὗ γὰρ κεῖθεν ἦλθε καὶ κράτη
sections.translation: No . Soon after he returned and found you ruling in Laius ’ stead ,

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ἐξικέτευσε τῆς ἐμῆς χειρὸς θιγὼν
sections.translation: he pled with me , with hand laid on mine , to send him to the fields , to the pastures of the flocks , that he might be far from the sight of this town . And I sent him ; he was worthy , for a slave , to win even a larger favor than that .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: πῶς ἂν μόλοι δῆθʼ ἡμὶν ἐν τάχει πάλιν ;
sections.translation: Would , then , that he return to us without delay !

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: πάρεστιν · ἀλλὰ πρὸς τί τοῦτʼ ἐφίεσαι ;
sections.translation: That is easy . But why do you enjoin this ?

sections.label: Section 767
sections.original: δέδοικʼ ἐμαυτόν , ὦ γύναι , μὴ πόλλʼ ἄγαν
sections.translation: I fear , lady , that my words have been rash , and therefore I wish to see him .

sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἵξεται μέν · ἀξία δέ που μαθεῖν
sections.translation: He will come . But I think that

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: κἀγὼ τά γʼ ἐν σοὶ δυσφόρως ἔχοντʼ , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: I too have a claim to learn what lies heavy on your heart , my king .

sections.label: Section 771
sections.original: κοὐ μὴ στερηθῇς γʼ , ἐς τοσοῦτον ἐλπίδων
sections.translation: It will not be kept from you , now that my forebodings have advanced so far . To whom more than to you would I speak in suffering such a fortune as this ? My father was Polybus of Corinth ,

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: μήτηρ δὲ Μερόπη Δωρίς . ἠγόμην δʼ ἀνὴρ
sections.translation: my mother the Dorian Merope . I was considered the greatest of the folk in that town , until a chance event befell me , worthy , indeed , of wonder , though not of my overreaction regarding it . At a banquet , a man drunk with wine

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: καλεῖ παρʼ οἴνῳ , πλαστὸς ὡς εἴην πατρί .
sections.translation: cast it at me that I was not the true son of my father . And I , vexed , restrained myself for that day as best as I could , but on the next went to my mother and father and questioned them . They were angry at the one who had let this taunt fly .

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: κἀγὼ τὰ μὲν κείνοιν ἐτερπόμην , ὅμως δʼ
sections.translation: So I had comfort about them , but the matter rankled in my heart , for such a rumor still spread widely . I went to Delphi without my parents ’ knowledge , and Phoebus sent me forth disappointed of the knowledge for which I had come ,

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: καὶ δεινὰ καὶ δύστηνα προύφηνεν λέγων ,
sections.translation: but in his response set forth other things , full of sorrow and terror and woe : that I was fated to defile my mother ’ s bed , that I would reveal to men a brood which they could not endure to behold , and that I would slay the father that sired me . When I heard this , I turned in flight from the land of Corinth ,

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ἄστροις τὸ λοιπὸν ἐκμετρούμενος , χθόνα
sections.translation: from then on thinking of it only by its position under the stars , to some spot where I should never see fulfillment of the infamies foretold in my evil fate . And on my way I came to the land in which you say that this prince perished .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: καί σοι , γύναι , τἀληθὲς ἐξερῶ . τριπλῆς
sections.translation: Now , lady , I will tell you the truth . When on my journey I was near those three roads , there I met a herald , and a man in a carriage drawn by colts , as you have described . The leader and the old man

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: αὐτός θʼ ὁ πρέσβυς πρὸς βίαν ἠλαυνέτην .
sections.translation: himself tried to thrust me rudely from the path . Then , in anger , I struck the one pushing me aside , the driver , and the old man , when he saw this , watched for the moment I was passing , and from his carriage , brought his double goad straight down on my head .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: οὐ μὴν ἴσην γʼ ἔτισεν , ἀλλὰ συντόμως
sections.translation: Yet he was paid back with interest : with one swift blow from the staff in this hand he rolled right out of the carriage onto his back . I slew every one of them . But if this stranger had any tie of kinship to Laius ,

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: τίς τοῦδέ γʼ ἀνδρός ἐστιν ἀθλιώτερος ;
sections.translation: who is now more wretched than this man before you ? What mortal could be proved more hateful to the gods ? No stranger , no citizen , is allowed to receive him at home , it is unlawful for anyone to accost him , and all must push him from their homes . And this — this curse —

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: ἢ ʼγὼ ʼπʼ ἐμαυτῷ τάσδʼ ἀρὰς ὁ προστιθείς .
sections.translation: was laid on me by no other mouth than my own . And I pollute the bed of the slain man with the hands by which he perished . Am I now vile ? Oh , am I not utterly unclean , seeing that I must be banished , and in banishment neither see my own people ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: μηδʼ ἐμβατεύειν πατρίδος , ἢ γάμοις με δεῖ
sections.translation: nor set foot in my own land , or else be joined in wedlock to my mother , and slay my father Polybus , who sired and reared me ? Then would he who judged these things to be sent down by some cruel divinity not be right about Oedipus ?

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: μὴ δῆτα , μὴ δῆτʼ , ὦ θεῶν ἁγνὸν σέβας ,
sections.translation: Prevent , prevent , you pure and awful gods , me from ever seeing that day ! No , may I be swept away from all men , before I see myself visited with that brand of doom .

sections.label: Section 834
sections.original: ἡμῖν μέν , ὦναξ , ταῦτʼ ὀκνήρʼ · ἕως δʼ ἂν οὖν
sections.translation: To us , king , these things are fraught with fear . Yet have hope , until at least you have gained full knowledge

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ παρόντος ἐκμάθῃς , ἔχʼ ἐλπίδα .
sections.translation: from the one who saw the deed .

sections.label: Section 836
sections.original: καὶ μὴν τοσοῦτόν γʼ ἐστί μοι τῆς ἐλπίδος ,
sections.translation: I have , in truth , this much hope alone : I await the man summoned from the pastures .

sections.label: Section 838
sections.original: πεφασμένου δὲ τίς ποθʼ ἡ προθυμία ;
sections.translation: And what do you want from him when he appears ?

sections.label: Section 839
sections.original: ἐγὼ διδάξω σʼ · ἢν γὰρ εὑρεθῇ λέγων
sections.translation: I will tell you . If his story is found

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: σοὶ ταὔτʼ , ἔγωγʼ ἂν ἐκπεφευγοίην πάθος .
sections.translation: to match withwith yours , I at least , will stand clear of disaster .

sections.label: Section 841
sections.original: ποῖον δέ μου περισσὸν ἤκουσας λόγον ;
sections.translation: And what special note did you hear from me ?

sections.label: Section 842
sections.original: λῃστὰς ἔφασκες αὐτὸν ἄνδρας ἐννέπειν
sections.translation: You said that he spoke of Laius as slain by robbers . If , then , he still speaks of several as before , I was not the slayer :

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ γένοιτʼ ἂν εἷς γε τοῖς πολλοῖς ἴσος ·
sections.translation: a solitary man could not be considered the same as that band . But if he names one lonely wayfarer , then beyond doubt this guilt rests upon me .

sections.label: Section 848
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὡς φανέν γε τοὔπος ὧδʼ ἐπίστασο ,
sections.translation: Be assured that thus , at least , the tale was first told . He cannot revoke that ,

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: πόλις γὰρ ἤκουσʼ , οὐκ ἐγὼ μόνη , τάδε .
sections.translation: for the city heard it , not I alone . But even if he should diverge somewhat from his former story , never , king , can he show that the murder of Laius , at least , is truly square with the prophecy , for Loxias plainly said that he was to die at the hand of my child .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: καίτοι νιν οὐ κεῖνός γʼ ὁ δύστηνός ποτε
sections.translation: How was it then that that poor innocent never slew him , but perished first ? From now on then , as far as divination goes , I would not look to my right hand or my left .

sections.label: Section 859
sections.original: καλῶς νομίζεις · ἀλλʼ ὅμως τὸν ἐργάτην
sections.translation: You are right . But nevertheless send someone to fetch the peasant ,

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: πέμψον τινὰ στελοῦντα μηδὲ τοῦτʼ ἀφῇς .
sections.translation: and do not neglect this matter .

sections.label: Section 861
sections.original: πέμψω ταχύνασʼ · ἀλλʼ ἴωμεν ἐς δόμους ·
sections.translation: I will send for him without delay . But let us go into the house : I will do nothing which does not please you . Exeunt Oedipus and Iocasta .
